New Feature request -
1. I want to add a checkpointing feature where the app saves the report on AWS S3 and then compares with the new report on next run.
2. On the next run, the app gets the last saved report, diffs with the current report
3. Final report, apart from the existing report also gives
    - Count and List of new resources added
    - Count and List of old resources removed
4. Then the new report overwrites the old report
5. Feature is controlled by a runtime config flag
6. S3 location is also provided by a runtime config value. Application should be able to take different S3 paths based on the env being DEV or PROD
7. S3 file is saved in csv format

Introduction to teams -
1. BI team supports reporting via Metabase which is connected to AWS Redshift. So skills would be related to building Metabase dashboards and querying data in Redshift to identify data required for reporting key KPI driven dashboards.
2. DWH (data warehousing) team read data from S3 and build analytical data models in AWS Redshift
3. Analytics team use data from Redshift to drive Digital, Marketing, commercial etc business related analytics
4. Data Engineering is involved in integration with various sources to bring data to the AWS S3 and build services on the data models exposed by Data Science teams and DWH team.

Use this information to build better project documentation and example skills

[Pasted text #1 +13 lines] review these code review feedback and make changes
/agents

Findings
•
High: scanner.py builds the query as -tag.key:team -tag.key:product and the surrounding comments assume that returns resources missing either required tag. Resource Explorer combines filters with implicit AND, so this only returns resources missing both tags. Anything missing just one required tag is omitted from the report. AWS docs: query examples.
•
High: template.yaml grants resource-explorer-2:ListResources, but AWS documents ListResources requires resource-explorer-2:Search permission. The deployed Lambda is likely to fail with AccessDenied unless that action is added. AWS docs: ListResources API.
•
Medium: scanner.py derives resource_type and name from the ARN instead of using Resource Explorer’s typed fields. That misclassifies several valid ARN forms, including S3 and SQS, and can leak the type prefix into the displayed name for colon-delimited resource IDs. AWS docs: ListResources API.
Assumptions / gaps
•
The first finding depends on the intended behavior being “report resources missing any required tag,” which matches the comments and CLI help. If the real intent is “missing all required tags,” then the implementation is consistent but the docs and naming are misleading.
•
The current tests are too mocked to catch the first two defects: they never exercise real Resource Explorer query semantics or IAM requirements.
Test appendix
I installed the local test deps into lambda/untagged-reporter/.venv and ran pytest in lambda/untagged-reporter/test. Result: 75 passed in 1.03s. That improves confidence in the internal code paths, but it does not invalidate the findings above because the suite does not cover AWS-side filter semantics or deployment-time IAM behavior. review these code review feedback and make changes

update @agents/deep-code-reviewer.md based on this feedback

1.content:
The latest review incorrectly flagged this as "the previous fix went the wrong direction" — it did not. The resource-explorer-2:Search value was correct. The agent hallucinated that
 resource-explorer-2:ListResources is a valid IAM action; it is not listed in the AWS service authorization reference. After this fix lands, consider updating the agent definition to always verify IAM
 action names against the AWS service authorization reference (not guess from API method names).

lets implement feature 1
- Add a new endpoint to API gateway which handles GET /status/{id} requests
- Create a new lambda for handling erasure requests called reporter_lambda
- Extend the @src/dns_de_rte_orchestrator/audit_logging.py to add methods for retrieving audit logs from DynamoDB for a erasure id passed from the /status endpoint
- If audit log is not available in DynamoDB, retrieval should fallback to reading from S3. Reading S3 location should be injected from SAM config.
- On S3, there will be 1 object per erasure_id, named <erasure_id>.jsonl.gz. Each object will have the complete audit log of the erasure_id
- API should return the audit log in json format ordered by retrieval order
- Add tests for testing these functionalities and negetive edge cases

pull and merge the latest changes from master

We need to build a feature to archive an erasure request on S3 once it has been successfully processed. The erasure request storage is called audit logs and are primarily stored in DynamoDB. We need to build a functionality which archives an ersure request to S3 on successful execution and deletes the request from DynamoDB. Request should only be deleted from DynamoDB once it is confirmed that the request has been successully saved in S3. There is an existing functionality which is already implemented which allows retrieval of these audit logs for a request from DynamoDB and if not available, gets the data from S3. This archiving feature should work seemlessly with that feature.
